query: Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo virus outbreak confirmed case count latest update late June and July 2026. Have any new cases been reported after 21 June 2026 bringing Uganda total above 20? Check ECDC CDTR Week 27, WHO Disease Outbreak News.
answer:
**Answer: No new Uganda cases after 21 June 2026. Uganda's total remains 20 confirmed cases (2 deaths) — it has NOT risen above 20.**

Both authoritative sources confirm this:
- **ECDC** (dedicated outbreak page, last updated 10 July 2026, data through 9 July): "As of 9 July 2026, in Uganda a total of **20 confirmed cases, including two deaths**... The last confirmed case was reported on **21 June and no new cases have been reported since then**. Sixteen individuals have recovered."
- **WHO Disease Outbreak News DON612**: Total 1,481 confirmed cases — 1,460 DRC, **20 Uganda**, 1 France; 454 deaths including 2 from Uganda.

The rapid case growth is confined to the **DRC** (1,792 confirmed cases / 625 deaths as of 8 July 2026), not Uganda. Uganda's trajectory is flat at 20 since 21 June. The next ECDC CDTR update was scheduled for 13 July 2026, so the 9–10 July figures are the latest available as of today (12 July 2026).

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda (DON612)
sources.snippet: In total, 1481 confirmed cases; 1460 from the Democratic Republic of the Congo, 20 from Uganda and one from France (linked to DRC); and 454 deaths including two from Uganda, have been reported.

sources.title: 2026 Ebola epidemic
sources.snippet: There have been 625 confirmed deaths in D.R. Congo, and two deaths in Uganda as of the second week of July.

sources.title: Bundibugyo virus disease updates - NICD
sources.snippet: As of 8 July 2026, health authorities had reported 1792 laboratory-confirmed cases (including 625 deaths), 227 suspected cases and 295 recovered cases in three provinces.

sources.title: Uganda Ministry of Health EVD Daily Dashboard
sources.snippet: Uganda Ministry of Health daily Ebola virus disease reporting portal (source cited by ECDC for 20 confirmed cases).

key_facts: Uganda's cumulative Bundibugyo virus (Ebola) confirmed case total is 20, including 2 deaths, as of 9-10 July 2026 (ECDC).
The last confirmed case in Uganda was reported on 21 June 2026; no new cases have been reported since then (ECDC, updated 10 July 2026).
Uganda's total has NOT risen above 20 — it remains stable at 20 confirmed cases.
Of Uganda's 20 cases: 15 had travel links to DRC and 5 were associated with local transmission events; 16 have recovered.
WHO DON612 reported total outbreak figures of 1,481 confirmed cases (1,460 DRC, 20 Uganda, 1 France) with 454 deaths including 2 from Uganda.
The DRC is where cases are rapidly increasing: 1,792 confirmed cases and 625 deaths as of 8 July 2026 (DRC SitRep N°055).
The outbreak is caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus and was declared a PHEIC by WHO on 16-17 May 2026.
A France imported case was reported 24 June 2026; a US citizen was medically evacuated to Germany in May 2026.
ECDC assesses the likelihood of infection for EU/EEA populations as very low.
Next ECDC CDTR / outbreak page update was scheduled for 13 July 2026; latest available data is 9-10 July 2026.

data_points.metric: Uganda cases with DRC travel links
Uganda cases from local transmission events
